title: asperula cynanchica var. scabra
intro: Asperula cynanchica var. scabra, also known as Woodruff, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Rubiaceae family. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a digestive aid and anti-inflammatory agent, and is valued for its health benefits such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues. The plant's leaves and roots can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or dried for later use, making it a versatile ingredient in traditional medicine. Woodruff has been used in folk medicine for centuries, and its preparations are still used today to promote digestive health and relieve various health complaints.
